title: The Anarchy of the 12 Warlords
summary: The country was divided into 12 different regions, with everyone wanting to be king, making life for the people very difficult.

slides.text: Did you know? After King Ngo Quyen passed away, our country fell into a very sad situation: the 'Anarchy of the 12 Warlords'.
slides.imagePrompt: Map of Vietnam divided into 12 colorful regions with different family banners, smoke rising from some areas, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: 12 leaders from different regions all wanted to compete for power, declared themselves kings, and fought each other. The country was split into 12 small pieces.
slides.imagePrompt: Different warlords arguing and pointing at each other across their boundaries, intense atmosphere but not scary, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: Wars occurred constantly, fields were abandoned, and the people could not live or work in peace. Everyone was waiting for a hero to appear.
slides.imagePrompt: A sad farmer looking at an empty field, a ruined fence, gray sky but still in cartoon style, children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.text: This was the greatest challenge since we regained independence, requiring a brave heart and wisdom to unify the land.
slides.imagePrompt: Sunlight breaking through the clouds over a divided land, a symbol of hope (like a blooming flower), children book illustration, cartoon style, bright colors

slides.question: Why is this period called the 'Anarchy of the 12 Warlords'?
options: Because the country was divided and contested by 12 leaders
Because there were 12 days of festivals
slides.answer: Because the country was divided and contested by 12 leaders

slides.question: What was life like for the people during this period?
options: Hard and fearful due to constant war
slides.answer: Hard and fearful due to constant war
